# terrible_f5networks_f5_modules_bigip_policy (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `name` (String) The name of the policy to create.

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `description` (String) The description to attach to the policy. This parameter is only supported on versions of BIG-IP >= 12.1.0. On earlier versions it is simply ignored.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `partition` (String) Device partition to manage resources on.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `rules` (String) Specifies a list of rules you want associated with this policy. The order of this list is the order they will be evaluated by BIG-IP. If the specified rules do not exist (for example when creating a new policy) they will be created. The `conditions` for a default rule are `all`. The `actions` for a default rule are `ignore`. The `bigip_policy_rule` module can be used to create and edit existing and new rules.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) When `state` is `present`, ensures the policy exists and is published. When `state` is `absent`, ensures the policy is removed, even if it is currently drafted. When `state` is `draft`, ensures the policy exists and is drafted. When modifying rules, it is required that policies first be in a draft. Drafting is only supported on versions of BIG-IP >= 12.1.0. On versions prior to that, specifying a `state` of `draft` will raise an error.
- `strategy` (String) Specifies the method to determine which actions get executed when there are multiple rules that match. When creating new policies, the default is `first`. This module does not allow you to specify the `best` strategy to use. It will choose the system default (`/Common/best-match`) instead.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
